notes: No Pickleheads page for Emeryville was found. Tried: site:pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/emeryville (returns no Emeryville-specific pickleheads.com URL, only third-party sites); a plain 'pickleheads Emeryville CA pickleball courts' search; an exclusion search for pickleheads.com + Emeryville minus neighboring cities; a literal-string search for the exact URL; and a direct WebFetch of https://www.pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/emeryville (HTTP 403, consistent with pickleheads.com blocking WebFetch generally, but no cached/indexed content for that path turned up via search either, unlike Berkeley/Oakland/Palo Alto/SF which all return real synthesized summaries). Treating this as Pickleheads genuinely having no Emeryville listing yet (a small, 1.2-sq-mi city) rather than a research gap. Exactly one real, currently-operating public venue was found: the City of Emeryville's own ECCL Gymnasium pickleball program. IMPORTANT GOTCHA, confirmed via multiple independent sources: 4230 Hubbard St, Emeryville (formerly 'East Bay Badminton' / 'Eastbay Badminton Association') is STILL listed as an active 4-court indoor pickleball venue by numerous pickleball aggregators (pickleballrush.com, pickleballplusapp.com, pickleballfire.com, globalpickleball.network, and courtsource-adjacent search results) -- this is stale data. East Bay Badminton permanently closed April 30, 2024 after 17 years (confirmed via The E'Ville Eye News, a local Emeryville news outlet: 'The pandemic hit us hard, and the financial strain has been real'; corroborated by Yelp's 'CLOSED' status tag on that listing and by eastbaybadminton.com now 301-redirecting to an unrelated third-party site, nattressplugins.com). A new operator, Pinnacle Badminton Center, opened in the same space in July 2024 (per The E'Ville Eye's X/Twitter post announcing its preview/grand-opening) and is confirmed still operating today -- but it is badminton-only. Checked directly against pinnaclebadminton.com's homepage, About page, AND its official drop-in program schedule page (pinnaclebadminton.com/drop-in, the most authoritative page for this question): zero mention of pickleball anywhere; the drop-in schedule lists badminton only, 7 days/week, via an electronic queuing system. One WebSearch AI summary claimed Pinnacle offers 'pickleball Wednesdays 10am-12pm for $5' but this could not be corroborated by the club's own site and is almost certainly leftover/uncorrected aggregator data from the East Bay Badminton era (the exact same stale-data pattern that produced the other incorrect 'active pickleball at 4230 Hubbard St' listings) -- not trusted, and 4230 Hubbard St is excluded from venues below. Also checked and excluded: 'Towers at Emeryville,' a residential apartment building (thetowersemeryville.com) that advertises a 'Fitness Center with Pickleball Court' as a leasing amenity -- no source indicates public or non-resident access, so treated as private and out of scope. Checked emeryville.org's City Parks listing plus individual pages/searches for Doyle Hollis Park, Marina Park, Christie Park, Point Emery, and Temescal Creek Park: none show tennis or pickleball courts (Doyle Hollis has only a half basketball court). The only tennis courts found within Emeryville city limits are both private: Pacific Park Plaza (6363 Christie Ave, condo HOA, 1 court) and Watergate Community Association (2 Captain Dr, HOA, 4 lighted courts) -- neither has any pickleball evidence, so both excluded. Also worth flagging: several programmatic-looking aggregator sites (globalpickleball.network, pickleballrush.com, swimply.com, mypickleballlessons.com) all repeat an identical-sounding claim of '18' or '15+' pickleball courts 'in Emeryville, CA' -- but where the underlying venue list is visible (globalpickleball.network's own fetched page), those courts are actually in Oakland, Berkeley, and Albany within a mileage radius, not in Emeryville itself. Treat any 'N courts in Emeryville' headline figure from these sites with suspicion; it appears to be radius-padded, not a real in-city count.

venues.name: Emeryville Center of Community Life (ECCL) Gymnasium
venues.surface: Indoor gymnasium floor (multi-use hard court), portable/temporary pickleball nets and lines set up for scheduled sessions
venues.price: $6 resident / $7 non-resident drop-in, adult 18-49; $3 resident / $4 non-resident, youth 5-17 or senior 50+; 10-visit pass $49 resident / $55 non-resident (adult), $24 resident / $29 non-resident (youth/senior) -- per the City's general Fitness Center / Open Gym Sports fee schedule, which pickleball is offered under
venues.hours: Pickleball: Saturdays 12:30pm-6:30pm only, ages 18+ (not offered Sun-Fri). City's own page noted a hiatus with play 'resuming June 20, 2026' as of this research date (2026-07-11) -- program appears to pause periodically (plausibly tied to the shared school-district calendar, since ECCL is a joint city/school campus) -- reconfirm current status before visiting.
venues.skill: Not specified (18+ age requirement; described elsewhere as welcoming 'players of all levels')
venues.weather: Indoor venue -- not weather-dependent. Emeryville itself is flat, bay-level East Bay wedged between Berkeley and Oakland, sharing their mild, fog-influenced climate: cool grey mornings especially in summer, rare frost, average highs roughly upper-60s to low-70s°F most of the year.
venues.notes: ADDRESS CONFLICT, RESOLVED: the City's own Pickleball page states the address as '1333 Park Ave, Emeryville, CA 94608' -- but that is Emeryville City Hall / the Building Division's address (confirmed via Yelp's 'City of Emeryville' municipal listing and a LoopNet parcel record both pinning 1333 Park Ave to city government offices, not to ECCL), almost certainly a copy/paste template error on the city's recreation subpage rather than a real fact about where the gym is. The actual venue is inside the Emeryville Center of Community Life (ECCL), a large joint city/school-district campus. Two independent pickleball-specific aggregators (courtsource.us and tennispickleballcalifornia.com) both instead give '1100 47th St, Emeryville, CA 94608', which is corroborated by Google/Waze listings for a generic 'Eccl' entry and for 'Jihern Volleyball Eccl' (another indoor court-sport group using the same gymnasium) sharing that exact address -- distinct from the adjacent ECCL Pool building, which has its own separate address (1170 47th St) and its own separate Google Maps entry. Used 1100 47th St as the best-supported address for the gymnasium. No geocode was found tied specifically to 1100 47th St, so lat/lon here are borrowed from the adjacent Pool building on the same block/campus (two source snippets gave 37.8355506,-122.28353 and 37.8354867,-122.2830584, within about 50m of each other) as a stand-in -- flagged approxLocation:true; a human should pin the actual gym entrance more precisely if exactness matters. PRICE CONFLICT, RESOLVED: courtsource.us describes the venue as simply free ('free to play... a Public facility'), but that listing is marked 'unclaimed' on courtsource's own page and reads like their generic default text for public facilities rather than a verified fee fact. The City's own Fitness Center & Open Gym Sports page instead publishes an explicit resident/non-resident fee schedule that pickleball is offered under alongside the other open gym sports; went with the official fee schedule over courtsource's default. GOOGLE MAPS CID: found a numeric-looking Google place identifier for 'Emeryville Center of Community Life Pool' (0xdfdd341999fbeaa4, at 1170 47th St) but that is a distinct Google Maps entry/building from the Gymnasium at 1100 47th St, so per instructions not to use a cid unless confirmed for the exact venue, googleMapsUrl is left null rather than reusing the pool's. RATING CONTEXT (not used as pickleheadsRating, which has no source and is correctly null): tennispickleballcalifornia.com shows 4.4/5 (73 reviews) and Birdeye shows 4.3/5 (80 reviews) for this facility, both almost certainly aggregate Google ratings for the whole ECCL pool/fitness complex generally, not a pickleball-specific rating -- included here only as color, not written into any structured field. Amenities list is sourced from tennispickleballcalifornia.com only (not independently confirmed by the city's own page, which lists no amenities for pickleball specifically) but is plausible for a full rec-center-with-pool building. Surface described generically per globalpickleball.network; tennispickleballcalifornia.com additionally listed 'Hard Court, Composite Court, Acrylic Court' as surface options, but that reads like a site-wide filter/category list bleeding into the page rather than a specific claim about this venue, so those materials were not used verbatim in the surface field.
